text.title: api-gateway-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-gateway-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Business logic in gateway plugins (Lua, Wasm, Lambda authorizers).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gateway becomes a deployment bottleneck and an invisible monolith; debt accumulates silently. Keep gateway thin — routing, auth, rate-limit, observability only. Push domain logic to services.

failure-mode.description: Gateway as orchestrator — using it to call multiple services sequentially and assemble responses with conditional branching.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tight coupling and bottleneck; use choreography or a dedicated orchestrator (Temporal, Step Functions) instead.

failure-mode.description: Single gateway as a single point of failure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Regional gateways and high availability are required. A single gateway instance fails the entire platform.

failure-mode.description: Wildcard upstreams — agents emit paths: ["/"] catch-all routes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly enumerate paths; catch-alls route unexpected traffic to wrong backends and make security audits impossible.
